title: Hotfix Train Priority Insertion
description: A release-and-rollout control workflow that manages promotion waves, rollback readiness, and deployment dependencies across environments. Focus case: hotfix train priority insertion.
assumptions: Promotion waves, environments and targets are pre-provisioned and represented as discrete objects.
Approval tokens and runbook checks are atomic and consumed or produced by single actions.
Automation agents and engineer roles are trusted actors with appropriate permissions for their actions.
Maintenance windows and compliance constraints are respected when scheduling promotions.
risks: Priority insertion can introduce incompatible dependencies if artifact/service bindings are incomplete.
Delayed approvals or missing runbook checks may stall the hotfix, causing missed SLAs.
Reserved targets may conflict with other concurrent releases, risking resource contention.
Rollback plans may be outdated if dependencies were altered during insertion, increasing outage risk.
Improperly validated canary segments can cause undetected regressions to reach broader user populations.
